docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Testing Infrastructure and Setup
docs.text: This document outlines the process and milestones completed to set up a robust testing environment for the Keyhole Automation Platform backend using pytest and related tools.

docs.title: ✅ Goals
docs.text:
• Ensure all backend test files are discoverable and executable via pytest
  • Align test file structure to mirror the source code structure
  • Validate that core systems such as the Qdrant vector DB and FastAPI backend are functional

docs.title: 🧩 Project Structure
docs.text:
Keyhole_Automation_Platform/
│
├── backend/
│   └── mcp/
│       └── src/
│           ├── server.py
│           └── ...
│
├── tests/
│   └── backend/
│       └── mcp/
│           └── test_server.py
│
├── init_project.py
├── pytest.ini
└── .venv/

docs.title: pytest.ini
docs.text:
# Keyhole_Automation_Platform/pytest.ini
[pytest]
minversion = 6.0
addopts = -ra -q
testpaths = 
    tests
pythonpath = 
    backend/mcp/src
    backend

docs.title: init_project.py
docs.text:
Ensures that the memory system and Qdrant integration initialize before any code is executed.

# Keyhole_Automation_Platform/init_project.py
import os
import sys

PROJECT_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
BACKEND_PATH = os.path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, "backend")

if BACKEND_PATH not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, BACKEND_PATH)

from memory_manager import init_memory_collection
init_memory_collection()

print("✅ Project initialized. All modules are now importable.")

docs.title: ✅ Test Results
docs.text:
Tests are now executable using the standard command:

pytest

All 7 backend tests passed successfully:

7 passed, 1 warning in 1.23s

⚠️ Warning noted from Pydantic v2 migration guide — safe to ignore for now.

docs.title: 🔄 Current Best Practices
docs.text:
• All test files reside in the tests/ directory
  • Test structure mirrors source code layout
  • All testable modules import cleanly using pytest.ini-defined pythonpath
  • Qdrant container must be running for memory-based tests to pass

docs.title: 📦 Coming Up Next
docs.text:
• Frontend test integration with React Native / Jest
  • GitHub CI/CD test runs
  • Test coverage reports via pytest-cov
  • Mocking external APIs (Vonage, Gmail, WhatsApp, etc.) for unit tests

🧠 We now have a stable and clean test framework that validates all backend functionality including memory, routing, and system health. Time to build on this.

docs.title: React Native Settup
docs.text:
✅ Setting up the React App (Vite + Tailwind)

✅ Frontend Testing (Jest-based)

✅ Matches your project's structure (Keyhole-Solution-App for frontend)

📘 React App Setup & Testing Guide ⚙️ Setting Up the React Frontend Our frontend is built using Vite with React and Tailwind CSS. The project resides in:

bash Copy Edit /Keyhole_Automation_Platform/frontend ✅ Installation Steps Navigate to the frontend directory:

bash Copy Edit cd frontend Install dependencies:

bash Copy Edit npm install Run the development server:

bash Copy Edit npm run dev Build for production:

bash Copy Edit npm run build Preview production build:

bash Copy Edit npm run preview 🧪 Frontend Testing Strategy We use Jest with React Testing Library for unit testing the React components.

🗂 Test File Structure All tests for the React app live under:

bash Copy Edit /Keyhole_Automation_Platform/Keyhole-Solution-App/tests Tests mirror the source structure from frontend/src. For example:

Component Source Test Location frontend/src/components/Chatbot.jsx Keyhole-Solution-App/tests/components/Chatbot.test.jsx frontend/src/utils/helpers.ts Keyhole-Solution-App/tests/utils/helpers.test.ts ✅ Running Tests From the frontend directory:

bash Copy Edit npm test Or to run with watch mode:

bash Copy Edit npm run test:watch If test:watch is not set, add it in package.json:

json Copy Edit "scripts": { "test": "jest", "test:watch": "jest --watch" } 🧪 Jest Configuration Jest config is located in:

arduino Copy Edit /frontend/jest.config.js We use:

jsdom as test environment

@testing-library/react for component testing

Support for .jsx, .tsx, .ts, .js

🧼 Best Practices Tests live outside the source code, grouped in Keyhole-Solution-App/tests.

Every component should have at least one unit test.

Use mocking for API calls or global stores.

Let me know if you want me to generate a starter jest.config.js or create an example test case in your structure.

You said: excuse me. I need this written in markdown for our mkdocs ChatGPT said: Absolutely! Here's the full Markdown version you can drop directly into your MkDocs documentation (e.g., as frontend.md under your docs/ folder):

markdown Copy Edit

docs.title: ⚙️ React Frontend Setup
docs.text:
The Keyhole Automation Platform uses a modern React frontend built with Vite and styled using Tailwind CSS.

📁 Project location: /Keyhole_Automation_Platform/frontend

markdown Copy Edit

docs.title: ✅ Installation
docs.text:
1. Navigate to the frontend directory: bash cd frontend

  2. Install dependencies: bash npm install

  3. Run the development server: bash npm run dev

  4. Build for production: bash npm run build

  5. Preview the production build: bash npm run preview

docs.title: 🧪 React Testing Setup
docs.text:
We use Jest along with React Testing Library for unit testing React components.

🧪 Test files live outside the source code under:

/Keyhole_Automation_Platform/Keyhole-Solution-App/tests

yaml Copy Edit

This allows for centralized testing and easier batch testing across all frontend modules.

docs.title: 📁 Test Folder Structure
docs.text:
Test files mirror the structure of your frontend/src directory.

Component Source Test Location frontend/src/components/Chatbot.jsx Keyhole-Solution-App/tests/components/Chatbot.test.jsx frontend/src/utils/helpers.ts Keyhole-Solution-App/tests/utils/helpers.test.ts

docs.title: ✅ Running Tests
docs.text:
From inside the frontend directory:

```bash npm test To run tests in watch mode:

bash Copy Edit npm run test:watch If test:watch isn't already set up, add it in your package.json:

json Copy Edit "scripts": { "test": "jest", "test:watch": "jest --watch" } ⚙️ Jest Configuration The Jest config file is located at:

arduino Copy Edit /frontend/jest.config.js We use:

jsdom as the test environment

@testing-library/react for component-level testing

TypeScript support for .ts / .tsx files

Aliased imports and babel-jest for JSX handling (optional depending on your setup)

✅ Best Practices 🧪 Keep test files outside the source tree in Keyhole-Solution-App/tests.

🧪 Every component or logic module should have a matching test file.

📦 Use mocking for API services, global stores, or environment dependencies.

🎯 Aim for unit and integration coverage, especially for form logic, API calls, and rendering.

🔧 Need help scaffolding tests or generating the Jest config? Just ask.

vbnet Copy Edit

Let me know if you'd like this broken into two separate docs (frontend-setup.md and frontend-testing.md), or want it auto-added to mkdocs.yml.
